title: Builder HVAC Coordination, Rough-In Requirements, and Construction Sequencing
scope: New construction and remodel HVAC integration: sequencing, rough-in coordination, framing provisions, clearances, code/inspection essentials, and load calculation inputs.
key_principle: It's cheaper to move a line on paper than a duct in a finished wall.

phases.action: Select system type (Split vs. Packaged vs. Ductless). Determine equipment location.
phases.critical_hold: Manual J (Load Calc) must be done BEFORE framing plans are finalized to size duct chases.

critical_hold_points: Chase sizes and joist bay penetrations coordinated
Outdoor unit locations coordinated with site plan
phases.coordination: Framing, electrical, plumbing, architectural all need HVAC input

phases.action: Submit Mechanical Plans and Energy Calculations (Title 24, REScheck).
phases.coordination: Ensure window specs on plan match energy calc inputs.

phases.action: Verify duct chases, equipment closets, and attic platforms are framed to size.
phases.coordination: Plumber must not run waste lines through designated HVAC supply/return chases.

phases.action: Install ductwork, boots, refrigerant lines, and set equipment.
phases.critical_hold: Do not insulate or drywall until 'Rough Mechanical' inspection is passed.

checklist: Photograph all duct paths, line set routes, and concealed drains
Confirm return pathways exist for every closed room
Confirm thermostat and grille locations match plans

phases.action: Install registers, thermostats, and outdoor condensers.
phases.coordination: Painter must not paint over thermostat wires or equipment labels.

phases.action: System startup, charge verification, air balancing.
phases.critical_hold: Must have permanent power. Gas meter must be set and unlocked.

electrical.thermostat_wiring: Run 18/8 wire (minimum 18/5) to cover future upgrades. Common wire is mandatory.
electrical.disconnects: Service disconnect required within sight of outdoor unit AND within sight of indoor furnace/air handler.
electrical.convenience_outlet: 120V GFCI outlet required within 25 feet of HVAC equipment for service.

condensate_drains.primary: Piped to approved location (hub drain, exterior splash block).
condensate_drains.secondary: Required if damage to building components is possible (attics/ceilings). Pipe over window or conspicuous place.
condensate_drains.traps: Must be trapped (P-trap) near the unit to prevent negative pressure issues.

refrigerant_lines.penetrations: Seal all penetrations through envelope with spray foam.
refrigerant_lines.protection: Nail plates required where lines pass through studs.

venting.combustion_air: If closet is sealed, must bring in two pipes (high/low) from vented attic or exterior.
venting.termination: Flues must terminate 2ft above anything within 10ft or follow manufacturer PVC instructions.

duct_chases.vertical_trunks: Minimum 20" x 20" clear space for typical residential trunk (2-3 ton). 14.5" stud bay is NOT enough.
duct_chases.soffits: Plan drop ceilings/soffits for horizontal runs if open web trusses aren't used.
duct_chases.key_rule: Size chases to accommodate duct dimensions PLUS insulation, sealing, hangers, and access.

equipment_closets.upflow_furnace: Minimum 30" x 30" (Standard). 36" depth preferred for filter access.
equipment_closets.door_width: Must be wider than unit width (typically 24"-30" door minimum).
equipment_closets.return_air_base: If unit is on stand, return air plenum needs 18-24" height below unit.
equipment_closets.key_rule: Mechanical closets that are 'barely big enough' become permanent warranty factories.

attic_platforms.service_path: Catwalk required from access hatch to unit (min 24" wide).
attic_platforms.service_deck: Level working platform (30" x 30") required in front of service panel.
attic_platforms.access_opening: Must be large enough to remove largest piece of equipment (typically 22"x30" or larger).

return_air_pathways.requirement: Every closed room needs a return path to prevent pressure imbalance.
return_air_pathways.hold_point: Do not drywall until return path strategy is confirmed for every closed room.

indoor_units.service_front: 30 inches clear working space in front of control panel.
indoor_units.combustible_clearance: 0 inches for most modern units, but 1 inch recommended for vibration.

outdoor_condensers.key_rule: Treat condenser placement like a breathing zone, not a storage nook.

water_heaters.relief_valve: Discharge pipe must terminate 6" from floor or to exterior.

common_failures: Missing nail plates on suction lines
Flex duct sagging more than 1/2 inch per foot
Disconnect blocked by unit or ductwork
Missing locking caps on refrigerant ports
No secondary drain pan/safety switch over finished areas
Combustion air not provided in tight spaces
Thermostat on exterior wall or in direct sun

duct_testing.requirement: Duct Blaster test required in most states (Title 24, IECC). Leakage often limited to 4-6%.
duct_testing.timing: Test at Rough-in (easier to fix) or Final (riskier).

fire_safety.fire_dampers: Required if ducts penetrate fire-rated assembly (garage to living, floor-to-floor multi-family).
fire_safety.garage_ignition: Gas appliances in garage must be elevated 18" above floor.

load_calculation_coordination.timing: Perform Manual J BEFORE framing starts. If windows change during build, re-run calc.
load_calculation_coordination.warning: Do NOT size based on 'Square Footage Rule of Thumb' (500 sqft/ton). Modern insulation makes this dangerously oversized, leading to humidity and mold.
load_calculation_coordination.recommendation: Contact ProCalcs for professional Manual J calculations: 772-882-5700 / tom@procalcs.net

common_builder_mistakes.impact: Crushed ducts, high static pressure, noisy airflow, reduced comfort
common_builder_mistakes.prevention: Require duct sizes early; frame chases with insulation and fittings in mind.

common_builder_mistakes.impact: Pressure imbalance, comfort complaints, doors slamming
common_builder_mistakes.prevention: Dedicated returns or transfer paths for every closed room.
Design closets for access and future replacement, not just initial fit.
Decide sealed vs atmospheric combustion early; coordinate makeup air.
Interior walls, away from supply air, sunlight, kitchens, exterior doors.
Lock equipment strategy before framing; treat changes as schedule-critical.
Separate dryer exhaust from outdoor unit location.

hot_humid_southeast.strategy: Unvented/Spray Foam attics preferred to keep ducts in conditioned space.
hot_humid_southeast.condensate: Secondary drain protection mandatory over finished ceilings.

cold_climates_north.strategy: Keep all plumbing and ducts out of exterior walls.
cold_climates_north.ice_protection: Condensers must be mounted on stands above snow line.

dry_climates.humidification: Rough-in water supply and drain for humidifier early.
dry_climates.filtration: Plan filter access; avoid restrictive filters without duct capacity.

coastal_zones.corrosion: Specify 'Coastal' packages or aftermarket coating for outdoor coils.

mountain_altitude.derating: Coordinate altitude derating for combustion appliances (~4% per 1,000ft).

trade_coordination.electrical: Verify disconnect location (within sight of unit) and wire gauge match HVAC submittal sheets (MOCP/MCA).
trade_coordination.plumbing: Condensate drains require air gap or trap primer connection; avoid conflict with p-traps.
trade_coordination.roofing: Coordinate roof jack locations for gas vents and bathroom fans early.
trade_coordination.insulation: Schedule duct test before insulation/drywall when possible.
